summary: Russia launched a massive combined strike on Kyiv using missiles and Shahed drones, causing civilian casualties and damage to cultural sites. Ukrainian forces reported downing 632 aerial targets, including 50 missiles and 582 drones. EU imposed new sanctions on over 80 Russian-linked individuals and entities.
key_points: Russia conducted a large‑scale combined missile and drone attack on Kyiv, hitting cultural sites such as the Kyiv‑Pechersk Lavra and the Dovzhenko film studio (Source: @eRadarrua/96814).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 632 aerial targets—50 missiles and 582 UAVs—across 42 locations, including Iskander and Kh‑101 missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/96809).
Four civilians were killed in Kyiv’s Obolon, Holosiiv and Solomiansky districts during the attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
More than 1,200 rescue personnel and additional rapid response units were deployed in Kyiv for fire‑fighting and rescue operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
EU added over 80 individuals and organisations, including lawyer Pavel Astakhov, to its sanctions list (Source: @tass_agency/380756).
Two rockets were reported over Kyiv’s Teploviy Complex 5 (TEЦ‑5) (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67775).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/65259).
Lockheed Martin cannot guarantee further Patriot missile supplies to Ukraine despite production plans (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30899).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive combined missile and drone strike on Kyiv, targeting cultural landmarks and causing civilian deaths. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 632 aerial threats, including 50 missiles and 582 UAVs. The EU responded with new sanctions on over 80 Russian‑linked entities.

points: Ukrainian emergency services deployed over 1,200 rescuers and rapid response units to combat 26 fires covering nearly 6,000 m² in Kyiv after the attack, with four civilian fatalities reported in Obolon, Holosiiv, and Solomiansky districts (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
The Ministry of Defense reported damage to Kyiv’s “Radar” plant and a UAV production workshop at the Dovzhenko film studio from Russian strikes (Source: @tass_agency/380766).
Ukrainian air defense units, including aviation, SAM forces, REB units, and mobile fire groups, downed 632 hostile aerial targets during the combined strike (Source: @eRadarrua/96809).
Four Ukrainian rescue workers were killed in Kyiv and one civil protection specialist in Kharkiv while responding to the attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV originating from the Black Sea heading toward the Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/65259).

Russian forces executed a coordinated strike on Kyiv using 50 missiles of various types, including Iskander‑M, Kh‑101, and 3M22 “Tsirkon” anti‑ship missiles launched from Crimea, Bryansk, Kursk and Vologda regions (Source: @eRadarrua/96809).
Additional attacks were directed at Dnipro and Kharkiv with missile strikes accompanying the main Kyiv assault (Source: @eRadarrua/96809).
Russian statements claim the liberation of the settlement of Artem in the Donetsk People’s Republic (Source: @tass_agency/380762).
Russia’s Nord Stream 2 AG filed a lawsuit in the EU court to contest the EU’s decision to reject Russian gas (Source: @tass_agency/380764).
Russian media reported the use of Tu‑160 aircraft launching cruise missiles from the first bomber deck (Source: @eRadarrua/96777).
Ukrainian sources reported UAV activity over the Shostka district of Sumy, heading southward (Source: @kpszsu/65246).

On the Sumy front, Ukrainian assault troops of the “North” group engaged in small‑arms battles in Bachyvsk, Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka and Novaya Sich, advancing up to 600 m (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30905).
In the Kharkiv region, Russian attacks caused fires and casualties, with four rescuers killed during response operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Russian forces claimed a combined strike on the city of Kharkiv during the same night, though specific details were not provided (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Ukrainian intelligence noted a UAV over the Dnipro direction from the west (Source: @kpszsu/65246).
Ukrainian radar reported UAVs over the Vyshorod district of Chernihiv region, heading toward Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/65244).
A UAV was observed over the western Chernihiv region, moving toward the Kyiv direction (Source: @kpszsu/65244).
Russian missile launches from the Crimea “TOT AR” area targeted northern Ukrainian airspace (Source: @eRadarrua/96809).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV over the Kyiv area during reconnaissance (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67807).
No ground engagements were explicitly detailed for the northern front in the provided messages.
A Russian attack on the Komyshuvakha settlement during humanitarian aid distribution wounded four people, according to Zaporizhzhia OVA (Source: @UkraineNow/81944).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV from the Black Sea heading toward the Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/65259).
No further specific southern front engagements were explicitly described in the source material.
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 632 aerial targets, including 50 missiles (5 Tsirkon, 15 Iskander‑M, 30 Kh‑101) and 582 UAVs of various types such as Shahed and Gerbera (Source: @eRadarrua/96809).
Russian missile launches involved cruise missiles from Tu‑160 aircraft and bomber‑deck launches (Source: @eRadarrua/96777).
Ukrainian reports indicated over 50 rockets and more than 400 UAVs were used in the combined strike on Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67809).
Potential MiG‑31K intercept missions were announced for the day, with alerts to be issued if needed (Source: @eRadarrua/96815).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV over Kyiv’s Teploviy Complex 5 (TEЦ‑5) (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67775).

key_developments: Massive Russian combined missile and drone strike on Kyiv, damaging cultural sites and causing four civilian deaths (Source: @eRadarrua/96814).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 632 aerial targets, including 50 missiles and 582 UAVs, across 42 locations (Source: @eRadarrua/96809).
EU imposed sanctions on over 80 Russian‑linked individuals and entities, including lawyer Pavel Astakhov (Source: @tass_agency/380756).
Four Ukrainian rescuers killed in Kyiv and one civil protection specialist killed in Kharkiv during response operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81926).
Russian claim of liberating Artem settlement in the DPR (Source: @tass_agency/380762).
Ukrainian assault troops engaged in small‑arms battles on the Sumy front, advancing up to 600 m in several villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30905).
Lockheed Martin unable to guarantee further Patriot missile supplies to Ukraine despite production plans (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30899).
sections.threat_assessment: Russia continues to employ large‑scale combined arms strikes, integrating ballistic, cruise, and anti‑ship missiles with swarms of Shahed and other UAVs, targeting both civilian infrastructure and cultural heritage in Kyiv. Ukrainian air defenses have shown high interception rates but the volume of attacks strains detection and response capacities. The ongoing sanctions regime adds diplomatic pressure on Russia but does not yet translate into immediate operational changes on the battlefield.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine mobilized extensive emergency services, deploying over 1,200 rescuers and rapid response units to mitigate fire damage and civilian casualties in Kyiv, while also reinforcing air defense assets across the country. Internationally, the EU expanded sanctions targeting Russian individuals and entities, signaling continued economic pressure. Western defense firms, such as Lockheed Martin, face supply constraints that could affect Ukraine’s air defense modernization.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of missile launch sites in Crimea, Bryansk, Kursk and Vologda remains critical for early warning of future combined strikes. UAV flight paths from the Black Sea toward Odesa and from northern Chernihiv toward Kyiv suggest expanded Russian reconnaissance and strike capabilities. Continued assessment of EU sanction impacts on Russian logistics and procurement networks is required.
